patches.find: The decay rates are real physical observations, but counting the same dataset five times as five separate confirmations inflates the win count without introducing new evidence.
patches.replace: The decay rates are real physical observations, but counting the same dataset five times as five separate confirmations inflates the win count without introducing new evidence. A further circularity: the dome's own monitoring code hardcodes the validation threshold at ≥28 nT/year — a value not derived from any dome-geometry equation but manually set in the model's configuration. Confirming that the observed SAA decay rate exceeds a threshold the model itself defined is not an independent prediction.
patches.rationale: ISS-475: The 28 nT/year threshold is hardcoded in raw-text/05-model.txt line 42 as a hardcoded model input, not a dome-geometry derivation. This creates a circular validation — the model sets the threshold and then confirms the observation meets it. Noting this strengthens the Misleading verdict.

patches.find: There is no dome-geometry derivation of why the ratio should be 2.26× rather than any other value.
patches.replace: There is no dome-geometry derivation of why the ratio should be 2.26× rather than any other value. Furthermore, the ratio is epoch-dependent: the dome does not specify the time window, velocity method, or coordinate system used to compute it. As the North Magnetic Pole has been decelerating and reorienting since approximately 2019 — a well-documented shift in pole speed — the ratio will shift over time. A number extracted from a single unspecified snapshot of a non-stationary trajectory is a historical description, not a stable falsifiable prediction.
patches.rationale: ISS-476: The 2.26x ratio is not a stable physical constant but a snapshot quantity that depends on the epoch of measurement. Without specifying the time window, it cannot be tested against future observations. This materially strengthens the Std Model Explains verdict.

patches.find: This ratio is extracted from published NOAA pole position data spanning decades.
patches.replace: This ratio is extracted from published NOAA/NGDC pole position data spanning decades.
patches.rationale: ISS-477: Link should point to the specific NP.xy pole position dataset rather than the generic NOAA homepage, allowing readers to directly verify the source data.

patches.find: standard core-mantle boundary dynamics predicts the same drift anisotropy without a dome
patches.replace: standard core-mantle boundary dynamics explains the same drift anisotropy without a dome
patches.rationale: ISS-478: IGRF/WMM models describe and explain the observed field anisotropy; they do not independently predict the 2.26x ratio. 'Explains' is more precise than 'predicts' and avoids implying the standard model independently derives the exact ratio.
